LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARILYN BESSEY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. CONTINUED TO MAY 18, 2020, PENDING HEARING OF THE PETITION TO CONTEST TRUST IN CASE NUMBER PTR-19-303227. NO LATER THAN MAY 11, 2020, PARTIES ARE TO FILE SIMULTANEOUS SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS EXPLAINING TO THE COURT WHAT ISSUES REMAIN. THE TRUST CONTEST AND DISINHERITANCE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT, PURSUANT TO PROBATE CODE 17000. IT APPEARS TO THE COURT THAT THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION CHALLENGED IN THE DEMURRER ARE RELATED TO THE ISSUES IN THE TRUST CONTEST. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, (E: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARILYN BESSEY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to May 18, 2020, pending hearing of the petition to contest trust in case number PTR-19-303227. No later than May 11, 2020, parties are to file simultaneous supplemental briefs explaining to the court what issues remain. The trust contest and disinheritance causes of action are within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Probate Department, pursuant to Probate Code 17000. It appears to the court that the other causes of action challenged in the demurrer are related to the issues in the trust contest. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: E.
